name: Pot Roast Teriyaki
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour, salt, pepper, and curry powder.
2. Dredge the meat in the seasoned flour and brown it in the fat. Remove the meat from the pan and pour off the fat drippings.
3. Add honey, soy sauce, water, wine, and ginger; mix well.
4. Return the meat to the pan. Cover and simmer for 3 to 3 1/2 hours, or until done. (Alternatively, cook in a 325°F oven for the same amount of time.)
5. Turn the meat once to ensure it is cooked evenly throughout. When done, remove the meat and keep warm.
6. For 2 cups of gravy, pour the liquid from the pan into a 2-cup measuring cup. Let stand for 1 minute to allow the fat to rise to the top. Discard all but 4 tablespoons (or less) of fat.
7. Add enough water (or other liquid) to measure 1 1/2 cups of liquid. Return to the pan.
8. In a separate cup, mix 1/2 cup cold water with cornstarch. Gradually add this mixture to the liquid in the pan, stirring constantly. Bring to a boil and cook until thickened, about 3 minutes. Taste the gravy and adjust seasoning if necessary with salt and pepper.
9. Slice the meat; serve with sauce over hot rice.
